{"input":[{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"channel-context","type":"separator","value":"Recent messages from this channel:"}"},{"role":"assistant","content":"Log this message."}],"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","instructions":"You are Looping AI, a Slack app that helps teams achieve their goals and coordinate work within a workspace or organization. Your Slack username is @looping. You are NOT ChatGPT, OpenAI, or any other named AI product — you are Looping AI. Never refer to yourself by any other name or suggest users search for or invite any app other than @looping.

All interactions happen through Slack. Every request you receive has been submitted by a user inside a Slack workspace — via a channel message, a direct message, or a thread. Tailor your responses accordingly: be concise, use Slack-compatible formatting (mrkdwn), and assume the user is reading your reply inside Slack. Do not reference other interfaces or assume the user is interacting through a web app, API, or any other channel.

If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information or capability required to complete a task or provide an answer.

Stay focused on the user's request. Provide helpful, actionable responses.

You are an organizational admin assistant. Your expertise covers workspace strategy, goal-setting, value streams, objectives, metrics, and team coordination. Help users manage and improve their organization.","tools":[{"type":"function","name":"echo","description":"Echoes back the input message. Useful for testing.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"The message to echo back"}},"required":["message"]}},{"type":"function","name":"web_search","description":"Performs a web search using the OpenRouter web search plugin. Returns AI-analyzed search results. IMPORTANT: Include ALL relevant context from the conversation in the 'query' parameter, as the search operates independently without access to conversation history.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"query":{"type":"string","description":"The search query with full context. Include all relevant background information, constraints, and preferences since the search tool doesn't have access to the conversation history."}},"required":["query"]}},{"type":"function","name":"dispatch_workflow","description":"Dispatches a workflow to the execution engine for administrative operations. Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes. The engine will execute the operations using its own tools and report the results. For most operations, pass an empty permits array. Only include permits for sensitive operations: 'deleteWorkspace' (requires workspace ID) or 'setAdminChannel' (requires channel ID).","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"workflowId":{"type":"string","description":"The workflow ID to execute. Use 'admin-v1' for administrative operations."},"permits":{"type":"array","description":"Optional permits for sensitive operations. Empty array for standard operations.","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"type":{"type":"string","enum":["deleteWorkspace","setAdminChannel"],"description":"The permit type."},"workspaceId":{"type":"number","description":"Required for deleteWorkspace: the workspace ID to delete."},"channelId":{"type":"string","description":"Required for setAdminChannel: the Slack channel ID to set as admin channel."}},"required":["type"]}}},"required":["workflowId"]}}],"store":false,"stream":false,"reasoning":{"effort":"high","summary":"auto"}}

In this conversation, the system says:\n\n\"You are Looping AI, a Slack app that helps teams achieve their goals and coordinate work within a workspace or organization. Your Slack username is @looping. ... All interactions happen through Slack. ... You are an organizational admin assistant. ...\"\n\nNow the user says: \"Log this message.\" This is ambiguous. The user wants us to log the message? Possibly they want the system to log the content of their message somewhere, maybe for the sake of note-taking. Typically in Slack, logging could refer to adding a message to some log channel, maybe via a workflow.\n\nGiven the instructions, we have a 'dispatch_workflow' function to handle administrative tasks, but we need to see the appropriate workflow to log a message. The instructions list \"dispatch_workflow\" is used for workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, session policy changes. It may also be used for logging?\n\nAlternatively, we might be expected to just acknowledge that the message is logged. But likely we should actually log it. In a real system, there might be a \"logMessage\" workflow. However, no explicit workflow ID for logging is given.\n\nThe doc says: \"Dispatches a workflow to the execution engine for administrative operations. Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes. The engine will execute the operations using its own tools and report the results.\"\n\nThe user says \"Log this message.\" That's not an administrative operation like workspace management. Could be considered an \"event management\" or \"log\" operation. Possibly we should check if we have a built-in workflow for logging to a log channel. There is no direct mention.\n\nNevertheless, the request: \"Log this message.\" Probably the user wants us to log their message in some central place. We could reply acknowledging and ask for specifics (like what channel to log). Or we could propose typical behavior: by default log to #admin channel maybe.\n\nGiven the ambiguous request, we could ask the user to clarify where they want it logged. Or we can assume the standard admin channel is used in the workspace for such logs.\n\nBut the tool dispatch_workflow expects a workflow ID like 'admin-v1'. That likely triggers some known admin functions. But we need permits for certain operations. Logging a message is not one of those.\n\nWe could just reply: \"Sure, I've logged your message.\" But if we can't actually log it, we could ask for a channel ID.\n\nGiven guidelines: If we don't know how to log, we should explicitly acknowledge that we lack the ability. But we could also attempt to request a workflow for logging, but no such ID is given. Possibly we need to ask for clarification.\n\nThink: The user likely expects that the system will log the message somewhere. The best thing is to ask for where to log it. Could also provide a standard approach: It will be logged to the #admin channel.\n\nBut the instructions say: \"Tailor your responses accordingly: be concise, use Slack-compatible formatting (mrkdwn).\" So a short reply. Maybe we should ask: \"Where should I log this?\" Example: \"Sure, which channel should I log this to?\" That's concise.\n\nAlternatively, we could check with \"dispatch_workflow\" with workflowId: \"admin-v1\". But we need to know the exact operation: there is no operation info needed; so maybe \"admin-v1\" can handle generic logs? Unclear.\n\nGiven the instructions: \"Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes.\" Logging a message is not precisely those categories. It could be considered \"event management,\" i.e., logging an event. So perhaps we can dispatch the admin-v1 workflow.\n\nBut the function signature for dispatch_workflow may require additional parameters like the action: but it only takes workflowId and optional permits. So we can't specify what to log.\n\nThus it's likely we need to ask for clarification (like channel ID or confirm where to log). Let's see. If the user previously set an admin channel, perhaps that is known. However we don't have that info. So we cannot assume.\n\nThus respond: \"Sure, I can log this. Which channel would you like me to log it in?\" Provide a minimal answer.\n\nAlternatively, we could respond: \"Message logged.\" But it's questionable.\n\nGiven the guidelines: If you don't know the answer or can't perform the task, and it's ambiguous, we should ask for clarification.\n\nThus respond: \"Got it! Where should I log this message?\" Optionally ask for a channel ID.\n\nBut should we include a code block? Probably not.\n\nThus final answer: (in Slack mrkdwn) \"Sure! Please let me know which channel I should log this message to.\"\n\nLet's output that."}],"summary":[],"format":"unknown"},{"id":"msg_tmp_p1iddymoty","type":"message","role":"assistant","status":"completed","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"Sure!
